speeches.name: Michael S. Barr
speeches.summary: Barr discussed consumer financial health metrics, indicating the Fed is monitoring household resilience and credit conditions rather than signaling an imminent policy shift.

speeches.name: Michael S. Barr
speeches.summary: Barr gave remarks on the balance sheet, suggesting continued attention to reserve management and liquidity conditions with no clear hawkish or dovish tilt.

speeches.name: Jerome H. Powell
speeches.summary: Powell said the FOMC kept the policy rate unchanged, emphasizing the dual mandate and the need for inflation to keep moving back toward 2%.

speeches.name: Jerome H. Powell
speeches.summary: Powell noted the labor market has cooled enough to support prior cuts but stressed that persistent inflation above target remains a concern.

speeches.name: Jerome H. Powell
speeches.summary: Powell reiterated that policy is not on a preset course, but the statement tone leaned modestly hawkish because rates were held steady despite inflation pressures.

macro_fred.narrative: Leaning Neutral: CPI at 4.0%, Fed Funds at 3.62%, unemployment at 4.3%.
